queries.prompt: How does the readLspTimeout function work in the explore hook?
What does df-explore-protocol.js do and what are its main exports?
How is config.yaml used for deepflow configuration?
How do hooks work in the deepflow codebase?
What is the ratchet pattern and how is it implemented?

root_cause_analysis.finding: Phase 1 always misses because claude --print has no LSP tools (documentSymbol etc.)
details: Without --model flag, claude --print uses default model which hangs trying to use tools it cannot access, hitting the 15s timeout
With --model claude-haiku-4-5 and --dangerously-skip-permissions, subprocess completes in ~5s but returns empty JSON array
LSP tools (documentSymbol, findReferences) are NOT available in --print mode — they require an active IDE session
The T5 spike showed hallucinated symbol data (model guessed plausible function names), not real LSP results
Fallback path (static template injection) works correctly for all 5 queries
constraints_discovered: CONSTRAINT: claude --print cannot use LSP tools — Phase 1 design requires fundamental rework
CONSTRAINT: Without --model flag, subprocess hangs (uses heavy model that attempts tool use and stalls)
CONSTRAINT: Hook must pass --model claude-haiku-4-5 and --dangerously-skip-permissions to avoid hangs
PROVISIONAL: Phase 1 could use Glob+Grep via --print instead of LSP, or use a different pre-fetch strategy
PROVISIONAL: Alternative approach — use the hook to inject a smarter Glob/Grep strategy rather than pre-fetched LSP symbols
